title: Slow Cooked Slovakian Walnut Skillet
description: A rich and crumbly one-pot dish featuring walnuts, this Slovakian skillet is low in glycemic index and perfect for a hearty dinner. It combines the flavors of walnuts, meat, and spices in a delicious emulsified sauce.
instructions: Heat a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the ground pork and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the smoked paprika, ground caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chopped walnuts and cook for 2 minutes, until they are lightly toasted.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for 20 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
